sources.notes: Hard version of inverse minimum partition.
Official editorial's hard part removes the quadratic enumeration with a compressed DP/greedy recurrence.

annotation.summary: Solve the inverse minimum partition problem under hard constraints.
annotation.constraints: The hard version requires near-linear or O(n log n) optimization over all partition states.
annotation.core_idea: Keep the same geometric/monotone structure as D1, but avoid enumerating every breakpoint. The greedy partition transition is monotone, so DP states can be compressed; only critical candidates on the envelope need to be retained. This yields all inverse answers by sweeping states in order and updating the best reachable partition count.

solution_variants.name: compressed-envelope-dp
solution_variants.summary: Maintain only critical envelope states and update the partition DP with monotone transitions.

tags.evidence: The hard solution compresses and updates partition states with DP.
The transition relies on greedy minimum-partition choices.
The same envelope geometry from D1 underlies the compressed state set.
